page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 12 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz, this instance utilizes the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, featuring 12 physical cores with a single thread per core, which eliminates resource contention associated with hyperthreading.

This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The KVM-hypervised architecture ensures secure virtualization. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance specifically for workloads that rely on high memory capacity and network-based storage rather than local disk I/O or parallel GPU processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r8i.3xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound applications without the overhead of paying for unneeded compute cores. This profile is suitable for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and medium-scale data processing pipelines that require substantial RAM but moderate CPU resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8i.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it provides dedicated physical cores with one thread per core under a KVM hypervisor. The instance includes a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing applications that demand significant memory capacity relative to compute resources.
